mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls signals robust labor demand, which historically precedes positive equity returns as corporate earnings stabilize and consumer spending remains supported.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with positive payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 0.5%, it indicates aggressive monetary accommodation that lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect synchronized consumer spending and business investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX returns are uncorrelated with the joint signal or show negative correlation when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained drop in industrial production below 95 signals persistent supply chain disruptions or weak manufacturing demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX recovers quickly despite low INDPRO, or the signal yields a positive Rank IC over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized rate reflects healthy economic expansion, supporting corporate profit growth and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: High GDP growth coincides with market declines due to inflation fears, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that typically precedes earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Market rallies despite rising unemployment due to policy expectations, or the signal produces a positive Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in CPI and FEDFUNDS indicate an inflationary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations quickly, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Market ignores inflation/tightening combo or rallies on growth expectations, resulting in a non-negative Rank IC over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a three-month consecutive decline in retail sales signals consumer spending exhaustion, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Consumer spending rebounds quickly or market decouples from retail sales, causing the signal to produce positive forward returns in >40% of instances.
